reviews: The sambal stingray was rather good. The sambal chilli was flavourful and truly the reason why everyone still comes back despite the price point. Stingray was fresh. And the meats slides off when picked with my fork. This is indeed the $18 large portion. I wonder what the small portion looks like. Even the sambal portion damn little. #StingyRay
Hotplate Squid. Super Q and tender. Not like typical rubbery texture. Just so super shiok eating them! Fresh stuff.
White Clams Lala cooked in sambal chilli paste, with its strong shrimp fragrance and sweet-sour undertones. Read more: https://www.misstamchiak.com/tokyo-west-coast/
BBQ Sotong @ my fav bbq seafood stall. *screams*
BBQ Sambal Stingray! Shiokness RED! Love Sambal!
BBQ Stingray

stall_name: A. Rashid Khan (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: The unusual Indian mee siam I've waited more than a decade to try. The main difference is the gravy is creamy and drinkable. Delicious overall, but I should've picked a better cut of mutton. I consulted the boss for fatty mutton and he gave me the leg cut, which was tendon not fats.

Chilling out at my favourite neighbourhood and having a plate of mee goreng (non-spicy) and kambing (mutton) soup at Ayer Rajah Food Centre, after running errands all-day today. This hawker centre is my family’s go-to place whenever we are craving for Muslim food. The satisfying and hearty mutton soup from A. Rashid Khan, has a good flavour that is not too heavy and intense. The best way to enjoy the soup is dipping the accompanied soft bread loaf to soak up the tasty mutton broth. YUM!

stall_name: Abdhus Salam Rojak (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: I don’t usually eat indian rojak but when i do, i go for the best one. This is somewhat like a legend in the West and it’s arguably the best in SG. What makes a good indian rojak is the freshness of the items and more importantly, the sauce.

best indian rojak i’ve ever eaten especially their dipping sauce that tastes a bit peanutty and it’s sweet and spicy. their items are also very fresh and crispy even after a long time. definitely recommended!
Best rojak !
Damn good Nasi Goreng Ayam. Wish the portion can be bigger. I did try to order drumstick but it was sold out.
Noodles were pretty decent, with a savoury sauce that wasn’t too strong. The highlight was definitely the roast duck, which was tender and well-marinated.
Wanton Noodles with Homemade Sauce in Ayer Rajah Food Centre. Roast Duck Drumstick Noodles - Glistening skin and tender meat.
roasted duck noodles 👍🏻 22.8.19

stall_name: Top Fried Rice (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: New hawker stall at Ayer Rajah Food Center run by a young couple. Their offering is King Fried Rice, quite similar to DTF style. This is their Garlic Fried Rice with Pork Chop ($5.50). The pork chop was tender and well-seasoned. The fried rice was fragrant but a bit oily.
dishes: Garlic Fried Rice with Pork Chop

stall_name: Wu Ba Ye Fried Hokkien Prawn Mee (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Probably one of the wettest hokkien mee I have had, Wu Ba Ye Hokkien Mee ($6) was unfortunately just decent at best. Although the prawns given were really fresh, huge and had a firm bite, the stock used somehow lacked the strong seafood flavour and was too sweet for my liking. Maybe the rest of the dishes they offer may be better!

First time to try this long time fried Hokkien noodles in Ayer Rajah Food Center. This is definitely the most wet and slurpy fried Hokkien noodles in Singapore. So much gravy that you can probably fill out half a rice bowl with the gravy. For $6, you get a decent portion of noodles with 4 Lala and two prawns, along with sotong and pork belly. This stall does not add lard bits but their lime was one of the largest I have seen. Unfortunately I didn’t get any hint of wok heat and the gravy was a bit too salty for me.
Fried hokkien prawn mee #umakemehungry
#opal #leaf #hokkien #prawn #noodles
Arrived at 12.30pm at the peak of lunch time. Although it took a while to get my order, it was worth it. The pork trotters were tender and the noodles were well-cooked.

reviews: One of the best prawn mee in the western part of Singapore, albeit their name says...
$5 for this bowl of prawn noodles. Meat is well flavored and oh so tender.
Definitely better than Beach Road Prawn noodles and many others we have tried.
Bee hoon mee dry with chilli. It came with three ...

Habib’s Indian Rojack. We had a dinner at hawker center for...
The journey to the West brings us to Ayer Rajah Food Centre which houses a stall opened by a young...
I love #indianrojak and for me, I usually goes to the #ayerrajahfoodcentre when I have craving for...

Came at lunch time and there was no queue. Ordered their Small Bowl Noodles for $5. It came with 2 big meatballs, 3 fishballs, some minced meat and slices of fish cakes. The noodles were cooked just right and the chili was good.

stall_name: Fried Cockles Kway Teow (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Ordered a $4 plate of Char Kway Teow and added $2 extra cockles. The portion was just right. The kway teow was moist and sweet, and the cockles were fresh.

stall_name: Shenton Way Power Stall (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Chilled out at my favourite neighbourhood and having a plate of mee goreng (non-spicy) and kambing (mutton) soup at Ayer Rajah Food Centre...
The noodles here are flavourful, spicy and satisfying. Good portion for the price.
Popular Prata stall.

stall_name: Jurong Fong Yuen Minced Pork + Fishball Noodle (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Was searching for curry mee on google & chanced upon Ayer Rajah Market's Fong Yuen, so decided to try. It was quite a wait at 12pm on a Sunday. The laksa gravy was rich and creamy, with a good balance of spices. The ingredients like fish cake and tau pok were fresh.
Bak chor mee #umakemehungry...
Let's have a bowl of laksa~...

stall_name: N.M. Abdul Rahim (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: The rice is fluffy, the flavour is robust and spicy.
Classic spicy red mee goreng.
Quintessential Singaporean supper food. Spicy by default if you haven't al...
